title: Emergency Obstetric Transport Access And Ambulance Coverage Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring emergency obstetric transport access gaps, linking health-facility location data, road-network data, ambulance-registry data, and maternal-mortality data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: The three-delays model identifies delay in reaching a facility as the second most lethal gap in maternal survival. Yet ambulance coverage data is almost entirely absent from sub-national health information in low-income countries. OpenStreetMap road networks and travel-time surfaces exist globally — linking these to where ambulances are actually stationed and where maternal deaths occur is the missing operational link.

success_metrics.target: Every access-gap estimate links facility, road-network, ambulance, and mortality data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
